name: Sierras de Pàndols-Cavalls – circular from Gandesa via Prat del Compte and Coll de Bot
name_original: Sierras de Pàndols-Cavalls – circular desde Gandesa por Prat del Compte y Coll de Bot
region_intro: Road cycling around Gandesa, located in the Terra Alta region of Catalonia, offers diverse landscapes for cyclists. The area features extensive agricultural and vineyard landscapes, providing scenic backdrops for rides. Mountainous terrain with challenging climbs and descents is present, alongside tranquil paths like the Via Verde de la Terra Alta, which follows an old railway line. River valleys and secondary roads contribute to varied cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Gandesa The most popular road cycling route is Sierras de Pàndols-Cavalls – circular from Gandesa via Prat del Compte and Coll de Bot, a 42.8 miles (68.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 34 minutes to complete. This difficult route navigates through the Pàndols-Cavalls mountain range, offering significant elevation changes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Som Pass – Cim de Caro Summit (1,441 m) loop from Gandesa, a difficult 80.2 miles (129.1 km) path. This route features a substantial ascent to the Cim de Caro summit, providing panoramic views of the surrounding mountainous terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Horta de Sant Joan – Via Verde de la Terra Alta loop from Gandesa, a 32.3 miles (51.9 km) trail leading through the Via Verde de la Terra Alta and the Canaletes river valley, often completed in about 2 hours 42 minutes. Road cycling around Gandesa is defined by vineyard landscapes, mountainous terrain, and greenways. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult climbs. The routes in Gandesa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 750 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Gandesa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Gandesa?
A: Gandesa offers a good selection of road cycling routes, with over 10 different options available for exploration. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate loops to challenging climbs.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Gandesa?
A: The road cycling routes around Gandesa feature diverse terrain. You'll find extensive agricultural and vineyard landscapes, providing picturesque backdrops. For those seeking a challenge, there's mountainous terrain with significant climbs and descents. Additionally, tranquil paths like the Via Verde de la Terra Alta offer smoother, relatively flat sections, often following old railway lines through river valleys.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Gandesa?
A: While most routes around Gandesa are classified as moderate to difficult, the region's &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/634983" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de la Terra Alta&lt;/a&gt; offers a smoother, relatively flat path that is suitable for all levels, including beginners and families. This greenway follows an old railway line, providing a gentle cycling experience.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Gandesa?
A: Experienced cyclists will find several challenging routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38239325" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Som Pass – Cim de Caro Summit (1,441 m) loop from Gandesa&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 129.1 km path featuring a substantial ascent to the Cim de Caro summit. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964462026/sierras-de-pandols-cavalls-circular-from-gandesa-via-prat-del-compte-and-coll-de-bot" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierras de Pàndols-Cavalls – circular from Gandesa&lt;/a&gt;, a 68.9 km route navigating through the Pàndols-Cavalls mountain range with significant elevation changes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass through scenic areas. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/634983" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de la Terra Alta&lt;/a&gt;, which winds through wooded areas and rural environments, crossing impressive viaducts. The greenway also enters the valley of the Canaletes river, showcasing imposing walls of conglomerates. Some routes offer panoramic views from elevated points like the Cim de Caro summit or pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1044274" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontcalda&lt;/a&gt; natural monument.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Gandesa?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964462026/sierras-de-pandols-cavalls-circular-from-gandesa-via-prat-del-compte-and-coll-de-bot" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierras de Pàndols-Cavalls – circular from Gandesa&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19829497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horta de Sant Joan – Via Verde de la Terra Alta loop from Gandesa&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options, allowing you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Gandesa?
A: Given its Mediterranean climate, the best times for road cycling in Gandesa are typically spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, making for ideal cycling conditions. Summers can be hot, and winters might be cooler, but the region offers year-round possibilities.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or food along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, cycling routes in the Terra Alta region frequently pass through traditional towns and culturally significant areas, which often have cafes and restaurants. A notable stop along the Via Verde is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/634991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bot Station Bar (Railbus)&lt;/a&gt;, offering refreshments directly on the greenway.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Gandesa?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.0 stars from over 20 reviews. Road cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, which include scenic vineyards, challenging mountainous sections, and the tranquil greenways. The variety of routes caters to different ability levels, making it an appealing destination for many.

Q: Can I reach the cycling routes using public transport in Gandesa?
A: While specific public transport connections directly to route starting points are not detailed, Gandesa is a regional hub. The Via Verde de la Terra Alta, for instance, follows an old railway line and connects small towns, suggesting potential access points via local transport options if available in those towns.

Q: Are there routes that offer panoramic viewpoints?
A: Yes, several routes provide excellent viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38239325" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Som Pass – Cim de Caro Summit (1,441 m) loop&lt;/a&gt;, for example, features a substantial ascent to the Cim de Caro summit, offering extensive panoramic views of the surrounding mountainous terrain. Additionally, the region's varied topography means many elevated sections of routes will provide scenic vistas.
tips: Bot is a very charming village, located in the heart of Terra Alta, in Catalonia. The region's Voie Verte passes nearby but the terrain is not suitable for road cycling, unless you have gravel wheels.
Quite easy and incredible road, definitely not to be missed!

The "Catedral del vi" or cathedral of wine is a modernist building built by a disciple of Gaudi, Cèsar Martinell, at the beginning of the 20th century. This is a cooperative that continues to produce wine, olive oil and other regional products. The visit is definitely worth it!

waypoints.name: Cathedral of Wine of Pinell de Brai
